pi_items.verified_value: 330.0m matches typical VLCC specifications; real VLCC TITAN (IMO 9293741) is 333m
pi_items.source: Web search: VLCC specifications from multiple maritime sources; Maritime Optima (TITAN vessel details: https://maritimeoptima.com/public/vessels/pages/imo:9293741/mmsi:613702404/TITAN.html)

pi_items.verified_value: 60.0m is the standard beam width for VLCC tankers
pi_items.source: Web search results confirm: 'the beam (width) is around 60 metres' for typical VLCCs; matches real VLCC TITAN at 60m

pi_items.verified_value: 21.5m falls within typical VLCC draft range of 20-22m; real VLCC TITAN has draft of 20.85m
pi_items.source: Web search: VLCC draft specifications typically range 20-22m; Maritime Optima (TITAN: 20.85m)

pi_items.verified_value: 340000 tonnes appears to represent displacement tonnage. Real VLCC TITAN has 299,097 tonnes DWT; displacement would be approximately 350,000-365,000 tonnes
pi_items.source: Web search: VLCC displacement ranges 300,000-365,000 tonnes; ship displacement typically 50-60% heavier than DWT; Maritime Optima TITAN specifications

pi_items.verified_value: 15.5 knots matches typical VLCC service speeds of 15-16 knots
pi_items.source: Web search: VLCC tankers typically navigate at about 15 knots; FRONT CENTURY VLCC has service speed of 15.7 knots with 15% sea margin

pi_items.verified_value: Most VLCCs feature a single main engine configuration
pi_items.source: Web search: 'Most VLCCs feature a single main engine configuration, though some specialized designs like the Stena V-Max feature twin-engines'; typical engines: MAN B&W S80ME-C series

pi_items.verified_value: 35 degrees is the standard maximum rudder angle for large vessels including VLCCs
pi_items.source: Web search: 'For ships like VLCCs and container vessels, rudder angles during course keeping and manoeuvring operation are limited to 35 degrees'; beyond 35 degrees propeller efficiency is reduced

pi_items.verified_value: Unclear - typical maneuver testing uses 0.5 deg/sec; 10°/min (0.167°/sec) for normal operations
pi_items.source: Web search: Rudder maneuver testing conducted at 0.5 degrees per second; normal operations maintain 10°/minute (~0.167°/sec) for safety

pi_items.verified_value: Unknown - this appears to be a simulation coefficient, not a real vessel specification
pi_items.source: Web search on propeller astern efficiency found conceptual discussions but no specific numeric coefficient of 0.3 for VLCCs

pi_items.verified_value: Unknown - this appears to be a simulation parameter for propeller walk coefficient
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed propeller walk exists as a phenomenon but no specific numeric values for VLCCs found

pi_items.verified_value: Unknown - this appears to be a simulation parameter for astern propeller walk coefficient
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed propeller walk exists as a phenomenon but no specific numeric values for VLCCs found

pi_items.verified_value: Unknown - this appears to be a Bridge Command simulation parameter, not a real vessel specification
pi_items.source: No real-world equivalent found; appears to be specific to the Bridge Command simulator

pi_items.verified_value: No registered vessel named 'MT Pacific Titan' found in maritime databases; appears to be a fictional vessel for training simulation
pi_items.source: Web search for 'MT Pacific Titan VLCC': Found similar vessels (TITAN IMO 9293741, PACIFIC TITAN IMO 9913224) but not the specific named vessel

privileged_info_summary: The task specifications for 'MT Pacific Titan' VLCC are based on realistic maritime data. Verified dimensions (330m length, 60m beam, 21.5m draft) align closely with actual VLCC tankers like TITAN (IMO 9293741: 333m × 60m × 20.85m). Displacement of 340,000 tonnes is consistent with typical VLCC displacement (350,000-365,000 tonnes). Maximum speed of 15.5 knots matches industry standards for VLCC service speeds (15-16 knots). Single engine configuration is standard for VLCCs. Maximum rudder angle of 35 degrees is the marine industry standard for large vessels. Simulation parameters (RudderSpeed 2.5 deg/sec, AsternEfficiency 0.3, PropWalkAhead -0.02, PropWalkAstern 0.05, BuffetFactor 0.2) are specific to the Bridge Command simulator and lack real-world equivalents. The vessel 'MT Pacific Titan' is synthetic/fictional, created for this training simulation task.
